generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance FrictionShadowInstance : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Identification element for a single digital shadow instance representing a one-to-one mapping between the physical PMSM axis and its digital counterpart in the reference model"
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance FirstAxisPositionEncoder1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, providing precise positional measurements for torque-current analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData FirstAxisPositionData
    base:isContainedIn SensingInfrastructure
]

instance FirstAxisPositionEncoder2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, providing redundant positional measurements for torque-current analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData FirstAxisPositionData
    base:isContainedIn SensingInfrastructure
]

instance FirstAxisCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) in amperes for the first axis PMSM motor, critical for friction loss analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
    base:isContainedIn SensingInfrastructure
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance FirstAxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisPositionEncoder1, FirstAxisPositionEncoder2
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol MQTTProtocol
    DTDFVocab:dataType PositionTimeSeries
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisCurrentSensor
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol MQTTProtocol
    DTDFVocab:dataType CurrentTimeSeries
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TorqueCurrentRelationshipModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model representing the relationship between motor current (I_M) and generated torque (τ_M) for friction loss analysis in the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance FrictionLossModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model capturing friction characteristics within the first axis PMSM system using state machines and analytical equations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcAutomatonEnabler
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Reference model aggregating contextual data traces from physical system for analysis purposes, serving as passive digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance PositionalDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series data representing positional measurements from the first axis, used for friction analysis and validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FirstAxisPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance MotorCurrentProfile : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed current data profile representing operational cycles of the first axis PMSM motor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message Queuing Telemetry Transport protocol enabling lightweight, publish-subscribe data transmission from sensors to digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine enabling behavioral modeling and validation of torque-current relationships"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralValidationService
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton state machine modeling framework for capturing friction-related behaviors in the PMSM system"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tooling framework aggregating and abstracting contextual data traces from multiple sensors for analysis purposes"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard-based visualization framework presenting aggregated data on friction coefficients and energy losses"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time friction detection service analyzing torque-current relationships to identify friction loss in the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionDetectionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAction
]

instance BehavioralValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Behavioral validation service using simulation and state machines to validate system performance against operational requirements"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceValidationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Friction analysis service processing current profiles to quantify energy losses and performance degradation"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyLossInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard-based visualization service presenting aggregated data on friction coefficients and energy losses"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalDashboardInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight identifying friction loss in the first axis through analysis of torque-current data patterns"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated action triggering maintenance alerts based on detected friction anomalies"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation insight confirming system performance against operational requirements through simulation and state machine validation"
]

instance EnergyLossInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative insight on energy losses due to friction within the first axis PMSM system"
]

instance PerformanceAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Technician action to adjust torque thresholds based on friction analysis results"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OperationalDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of friction coefficients and energy losses for operational decision-making"
]

// C1: Environment
instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Component-based Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 first axis PMSM system"
    base:contains FrictionShadowInstance, SensingInfrastructure
]

instance SensingInfrastructure : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical sensing infrastructure comprising position encoders and current sensors for the first axis"
    base:contains FirstAxisPositionEncoder1, FirstAxisPositionEncoder2, FirstAxisCurrentSensor
]

// C5: Components
instance FirstAxisPMSMMotor : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) representing the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, critical for torque generation and friction analysis"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalSystem
]

instance PhysicalSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Serial robot MABI MAX 100 first axis system comprising electrical (motor) and mechanical (friction) subsystems"
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

// Additional required instances for dataType and transmissionProtocol
instance PositionTimeSeries : DTDFVocab:DataType [
    base:desc "Time series data type representing positional measurements"
]

instance CurrentTimeSeries : DTDFVocab:DataType [
    base:desc "Time series data type representing current measurements"
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling data transmission between physical sensors and digital twin"
]

    base:desc "
    The document does not explicitly describe **control flows** from digital to physical systems. However, it implies:

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates within a **real-time, data-driven** temporal framework, leveraging continuous synchronization between physical and digital components. Sensor data streams are ingested in **live mode**, with time-series data for robot joint behavior and friction models dynamically updated via **real-time data ingestion pipelines**. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz or ms intervals) are not detailed, the system inherently relies on **low-latency data flows** to maintain coherence between physical and digital representations. The integration of **white-box and black-box friction models** with live data underscores the need for **temporal alignment** at the granularity of operational events (e.g., joint movements or sensor readings), though precise time-scale specifications (e.g., microsecond-level precision) are not provided in the documents. The system’s reliance on **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., C++, Java) for behavioral modeling further implies the necessity of **time-aware execution** to ensure consistency across abstraction layers."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the documents is framed within a **reference model for Digital Shadows** (Fig. 1), which defines a **UML-based class diagram** to describe key concepts like data aggregation, abstraction, and contextual traces. The system’s orchestration relies on **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) to manage **functional and logical architecture** via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like SysML, AADL, or MontiArc. **Component integration** is achieved through **component-connector models**, where systems are described from a **perspective of modularity, interfaces, and interconnections**. **Resource management** is implied by the need to handle **heterogeneous data sources** (e.g., sensors, simulations) and **stakeholder-specific perspectives**, but no explicit **orchestration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., middleware, APIs) are detailed. The document emphasizes **modeling abstraction layers** (requirements, functions, logic, physics) but does not specify how these are dynamically coordinated in a real-time Digital Twin system."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The transition between these stages is governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM (Object-Oriented Systems Engineering Method)**, **SPES (Systematic Process for Engineering Systems)**, and **SMArDT (Smart Digital Twin Methodology)**. Each stage employs distinct **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**, **ACME (Architecture Description Language)**, **AADL (Avionics and Automotive Open Systems Architecture Language)**, and **MontiArc** for component-connector modeling, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavior modeling is further refined using **SysML state machines (v1.7)**, **MontiArc Automaton**, or integration with simulation tools such as **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++/Java**). Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool model coordination**, ensuring alignment across disparate modeling notations and tools. Transition management between stages adheres to **structured MBSE workflows**, where each layer builds upon prior abstractions to construct a cohesive digital representation of the physical system. Governance frameworks emphasize **traceability, consistency, and interoperability** across lifecycle phases, though explicit lifecycle governance approaches (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are not detailed in the provided documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s **fidelity** is ensured through **comparative validation** against **reference values** (e.g., simulated vs. measured positional data), aligning with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies. **Uncertainty sources** are quantified via **positional uncertainty analysis**, addressing deviations between simulated and real-world behavior. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are implied through **data aggregation and abstraction pipelines**, though explicit protocols (e.g., statistical filtering, model recalibration) are not detailed. **Quality assurance** processes include **cross-validation with simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) and **comparative analysis of time-series data** (streamed via MQTT) to mitigate discrepancies. The system leverages **architecture description languages (ADLs)** and **state machines** for behavioral consistency checks, ensuring alignment between logical and physical system representations. However, specific **Verification & Validation (V&V) protocols** (e.g., formal methods, statistical testing) are not explicitly outlined beyond general MBSE principles.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document does not provide specific details about the **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system. While it references the use of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** and **modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink) for system behavior, no mention is made of communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, TCP/IP), network topology (e.g., publish-subscribe, peer-to-peer), bandwidth/latency requirements, or security protocols. The Digital Shadow Reference Model [32] focuses on the **one-way data flow** and does not address bidirectional or control-oriented communication. Thus, no technical specifications for network architecture, reliability mechanisms (e.g., redundancy, QoS), or interoperability standards are available."
]

    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems** via **MQTT-based data streaming protocols**, as explicitly stated: *'We have access to time series of all sensors, which are streamed via MQTT'*. instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly detail data ownership policies, privacy protection measures, or compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, CCPA) for Digital Twin systems. However, the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (as described in [46], [47], and [50]) establishes a structured metadata model for asset management, including **UID-based identification** and **semantic descriptions** of assets. While these documents reference the AAS as a standardized approach for **asset administration**, they do not specify mechanisms for **data ownership governance**, **consent management**, **anonymization techniques**, or **data sharing agreements**. The **Platform Industrie 4.0** documentation ([50]) emphasizes the AAS's role in **traceability and interoperability** but does not address privacy or ownership controls. Thus, **no concrete details on data governance or privacy measures** are extractable from the provided documents."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The documents reference **cybersecurity protocols** and **safety mechanisms** in Digital Twin systems, though with limited technical specificity. **[42]** discusses the application of **Digital Shadows** across the **automation pyramid**, implying **real-time monitoring** and **data integrity validation** but does not detail **access control mechanisms** or **authentication systems**. **[52]** mentions the use of **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for communication, which inherently supports **QoS levels (0-2)** for reliability and security, though no specific QoS configuration is cited. **[44]** (MABI robot) describes a **safety cell** for robotic operations, indicating **physical safety measures** but no explicit **fail-safe protocols** or **collision detection systems**. **[46]** and **[50]** reference the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** as a framework for **asset management**, which could theoretically underpin **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** or **attribute-based access control (ABAC)**, but no implementation details are provided. **[49]** (RAMI 4.0) outlines a **reference architecture** for Industrie 4.0, including **security layers**, but does not specify **threat mitigation strategies** or **risk assessment methodologies**. Thus, while **MQTT-based communication** and **safety cells** are mentioned, **no detailed cybersecurity protocols**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **safety-critical system considerations** are explicitly described in the documents."
]
